ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for a chickpea-pea-oat-corn patty over jasmine rice with tamarind glaze. FOODON_00002809 (rice dish) used as the closest defensible ancestor class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (rice-based composite dish) used as the closest defensible match. No specific code exists for a vegetarian chickpea-patty rice bowl with tamarind glaze.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for this composite dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, comprising a chickpea-pea-oat-corn patty over a bed of jasmine rice with tomato sauce, tamarind glaze, and crispy fried shallots.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo — Groovy Veggie Khao (Vegetarian), vendor-reported nutrition panel; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for chickpea and legume composite estimation
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy 55 kcal, protein 10.7 g, fat 0.0 g, carbohydrate 2.36 g per 100 g) are taken directly from the HPB Foodo vendor-reported panel for this dish. These values are nutritionally atypical for a rice-based dish and are flagged as potentially reflecting a partial or patty-only portion rather than the full plated serving. Secondary nutrients (dietary fibre, sodium, saturated fat, sugar) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy chickpea and legume composite data (USDA FDC IDs 173757, 175237) and scaled to the reported macro profile. Fat_g is reported as 0.0 g by the HPB source; this is confirmed zero per the vendor nutrition panel. All estimated secondary values are marked with reduced confidence.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; jasmine rice GI reference (GI ~64–72) moderated by chickpea-legume component (GI ~28–36) yielding blended dish estimate of ~56
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. GI estimated as a weighted blend: jasmine rice (GI ~68, University of Sydney GI Database) contributes approximately 50% of carbohydrate mass; chickpea patty components (chickpeas GI ~28, green peas GI ~48, oats GI ~55) contribute the remainder. Weighted mean yields approximately GI 56 (medium). Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (56 × 8.3) / 100 ≈ 4.6, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 2.36 × 3.5 = 8.26 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Estimated sodium ~290 mg/100 g from tamarind glaze, tomato sauce, and fried shallot preparation
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium sauce options and avoid adding table condiments, as the combined sodium from tamarind glaze and tomato sauce may approach 1,000 mg per full serving; potassium from chickpeas and peas (~220 mg/100 g) provides partial DASH-diet offset.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low reported carbohydrate (2.36 g/100 g), high protein (10.7 g/100 g), and estimated dietary fibre (~2.5 g/100 g) from chickpeas and oats support glycaemic control
type2_diabetes.guidance: The chickpea-oat patty base provides soluble fibre and plant protein that slow glucose absorption; however, if jasmine rice constitutes a large portion of the serving, actual carbohydrate load will be substantially higher than the reported 2.36 g/100 g, and individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request a smaller rice portion or substitute brown rice to lower the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero reported fat and saturated fat, with plant-based protein from chickpeas and peas supporting a cardioprotective dietary pattern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The absence of animal fat and the legume-dominant protein base align with a heart-healthy dietary pattern; however, sodium from the tamarind glaze and tomato sauce should be monitored, and the nut allergen declaration warrants caution for individuals with nut-related cardiovascular risk factors.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Groovy Veggie Khao (Vegetarian) is a Singapore café-style vegetarian rice bowl dish featuring a pan-formed patty made from chickpeas, green peas, rolled oats, and corn, served over a bed of steamed jasmine rice. The dish is finished with a tangy tomato-based sauce and a tamarind glaze, then topped with crispy fried shallots for textural contrast. The flavour profile combines the earthy nuttiness of chickpeas and oats with the sweet-sour complexity of tamarind and the umami depth of tomato sauce. The dish is labelled as containing nuts. Per 100 g, the vendor-reported nutrition panel records 55 kcal, 10.7 g protein, 0.0 g fat, and 2.36 g carbohydrate — values that are atypically low for a rice-based dish and likely reflect the patty component or a partial portion rather than the full plated serving. Estimated dietary fibre is approximately 2.5 g/100 g from the legume and oat base. The dish is suitable for vegetarians and is dairy-free. Clinically, the high plant protein and fibre content support glycaemic control and cardiovascular health, while the sodium contribution from the sauces warrants attention for individuals managing hypertension. The dish is positioned as a wholesome, plant-forward meal option appropriate for lunch or dinner in a Singaporean café context.

confidence_reason: Core macros are taken directly from the HPB Foodo vendor-reported nutrition panel (confidence 0.95 per input), but the reported values (55 kcal, 10.7 g protein, 0.0 g fat, 2.36 g carbohydrate per 100 g) are nutritionally atypical for a rice-based dish and may reflect a partial portion or patty-only measurement. Secondary nutrients are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy legume composites and carry lower confidence. GI is an analogous estimate. Overall confidence is rated medium pending vendor verification of the full-serving nutrition profile.
notes: ZERO-MACRO JUSTIFICATION: fat_g is reported as 0.0 g per 100 g. This value is confirmed zero per the HPB Foodo vendor nutrition panel for this dish. The patty is formed from chickpeas, peas, oats, and corn without added oil reported, and the tamarind glaze and tomato sauce are low-fat preparations; confirmed zero per HPB source. saturated_fat_g is 0.0 g as a direct consequence of zero total fat; confirmed zero by formulation. trans_fat_g is 0.0 g; naturally zero given the plant-based, non-hydrogenated ingredient profile. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 mg; naturally zero as this is a fully plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in HPB Foodo panel or USDA FDC for this specific dish formulation. calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data not reported in the HPB Foodo vendor panel for this dish, and no sufficiently close composite entry exists in USDA FDC SR Legacy to support reliable estimation for these fields. NUTRITIONAL ANOMALY FLAG: The reported macros (55 kcal, 10.7 g protein, 0.0 g fat, 2.36 g carbohydrate per 100 g) are inconsistent with a typical rice-based dish and may reflect the patty component only or a data entry anomaly. Vendor verification is strongly recommended before clinical use. CUISINE NOTE: Input record classifies this dish as Japanese cuisine; however, the dish name, ingredients (tamarind, jasmine rice, fried shallots), and vendor context (Singapore HPB Foodo) are consistent with a Singaporean café-fusion dish. The Japanese local-language name has been provided as required by the G6 rule for the declared cuisine, but the actual cultural origin is assessed as Singaporean café-fusion. ALLERGEN NOTE: Dish is declared to contain nuts; specific nut type not identified in the description.
